# Joseph Mattiello

> Engineering Leader and Mobile Architect with 17+ years of iOS experience. Creator of Provenance EMU (the most popular open-source multi-system emulator for iOS, tvOS, and macOS). Currently on Wayfair's App Infrastructure team. Holds 3 US patents from Sense Networks. Builds production AI tooling using Claude, Cursor, and custom MCP servers. Based in New York City.

## Quick facts

- **Role**: L4 Mobile Engineer, App Infrastructure at Wayfair (2025–present)
- **Prior**: Director, iOS Platform at NewsCorp (8-region global team); Principal iOS Engineer at Cameo; Lead Mobile Architect at Hearst; Software Engineer at Sense Networks (3 US patents named inventor)
- **Location**: New York, NY (open to remote)
- **Availability**: Considering 1–2 consulting engagements for Q3 2026; selectively open to Head of AI Platform, VP Engineering, Director, and CTO roles
- **Contact**: mail@joemattiello.com
- **Site**: https://joemattiello.dev

## Featured projects

- [Provenance](https://provenance-emu.com): Multi-system retro game emulator for iOS, tvOS, and macOS. 6,304 GitHub stars, 79 contributors, 9,373 Discord members, 388,000+ GitHub release downloads. Launched on Apple App Store in January 2025. Supports 50+ console systems through 60+ emulation cores. Source: https://github.com/Provenance-Emu/Provenance
- [Virtual Jaguar (libretro)](https://github.com/libretro/virtualjaguar-libretro): Maintainer. Delivered the first open-source Atari Jaguar CD emulation. 28 merged PRs.
- [iCube](https://icube-emu.com): GameCube/Wii emulator for iOS using a JIT-less interpreter optimized for Apple Silicon.
- [iFly](https://ifly-emu.com): Dreamcast emulator for iOS using a JIT-less GPU/CPU interpreter for Apple Silicon.
- [Jaguar 240p Test Suite](https://github.com/JoeMatt/atari_jaguar_240p_test_suite): Port of Artemio Urbina's 240p Test Suite to Atari Jaguar — bare-metal m68k+RISC platform with effectively no LLM training data. Built end-to-end with Claude Code as primary author + Copilot and Qodo as adversarial reviewers. 36 PRs merged in 10 days, ~84% with explicit AI authorship. Shipped 12 tagged releases (v0.6.4 → v1.3.1) including full PAL/NTSC region switching, 7 hardware probes (93C46 EEPROM read/write, Jaguar CD/Butch, JagLink, TOM registers), and a libretro-based smoke-test CI that boots every PR's ROM and uploads the framebuffer as an artifact.

## Open source contributions

- [RetroArch (libretro)](https://github.com/libretro/RetroArch): Authored Apple platform drivers — camera (AVFoundation), microphone, CoreMIDI, and CoreLocation — merged to mainline iOS, macOS, and tvOS builds. 4 merged PRs.
- [Hero Transitions](https://github.com/HeroTransitions/Hero): Maintainer of the popular custom view-controller transition library for Swift (22k+ stars).
- [SideStore](https://github.com/SideStore/SideStore): Org member. 21 merged PRs covering Rust build integration, error handling, settings, and pairing.
- [RxSwift Community](https://github.com/RxSwiftCommunity/RxRealm): Maintainer of RxRealm and RxReachability.
- [swift-corelibs-foundation](https://github.com/swiftlang/swift-corelibs-foundation): Contributor to Apple's official Swift open-source standard library.
- [UTM](https://github.com/utmapp/UTM): Contributed iOS AltKit support for the popular VM app.
- [Mono / Xamarin](https://www.mono-project.com/): Authored CoreMIDI, CoreAudio, and Foundation C# wrappers for MonoMac.

## Patents

Three US patents from Sense Networks (acquired twice — by YP/YellowPages in 2014, then by Verve in 2017):

- [US8224766B2](https://patents.google.com/patent/US8224766B2): Comparing spatial-temporal trails in location analytics
- [US8620624B2](https://patents.google.com/patent/US8620624B2): Event identification in sensor analytics
- [US9571962B2](https://patents.google.com/patent/US9571962B2): System and method of performing location analytics

## Case studies (selected)

- **Wayfair — ~$300k/year bandwidth saved in two days.** MP4 compression and caching strategy across video assets. Plus SwiftUI purchase-flow optimization and Datadog RUM bootup instrumentation. Custom MCP servers and Claude skills/plugins for the platform team.
- **NewsCorp — From iOS lagging Android to surpassing it.** Director of iOS Platform across NewsCorp brands (Wall Street Journal, NY Post, The Times UK, Knewz). Built shared iOS Platform SDK as "internal open source" model. Restructured and hired across 8 regions. Modernized CI/CD with GitHub Actions and Jira automation. Shipped Knewz from scratch.
- **Cameo — Native HD video on a React Native stack.** First native iOS engineer on the team. Rebuilt iOS RN build pipeline. Shipped HD video as a native React Native module via AVFoundation.
- **Multi-Agent AI CI Pipeline (open source).** Built a production CI ecosystem across the Provenance organization that orchestrates three autonomous agents — Claude (Sonnet + Opus, auto-selected by static diff analysis), Cursor Agent, and Kimi Code — sharing one dispatch vocabulary. Includes diff-aware Claude Haiku localization, scheduled Copilot review polling that bypasses GitHub's bot-actor approval gate, and self-healing CI on the wiki repo.

## Consulting

[Mobile AI Launch Readiness Audit](https://joemattiello.dev/consulting/) — 3-week fixed-fee engagement, $25,000–$35,000. Covers iOS performance, AI integration architecture, App Store review readiness, and on-device LLM/MLX/Core ML pipelines. Deliverable: written report plus 60-minute readout. Niche: iOS engineering meets AI infrastructure.

## Skills

- **Languages**: Swift (5/5), Objective-C (5/5), C++ (4/5), C (5/5), JavaScript (4/5), Python (3/5), C# (3/5)
- **iOS / Apple SDKs**: UIKit, SwiftUI, Core Audio, AVFoundation, Combine, XCTest, Realm, MLX, Core ML
- **AI / LLM tooling**: Claude Code, Cursor, MCP Servers, Anthropic API, model-routing heuristics, agent eval
- **Platform / DevOps**: GitHub Actions, Datadog RUM, Jira automation, n8n, Fastlane, CI/CD
- **Architecture**: Mobile platform SDKs, multi-region engineering, performance engineering

## Education

BS Computer Science, University at Buffalo (2004–2008)

## Writing

Long-form writing at https://joemattiello.dev/blog/. Recent:
- 2026-05-01 — [The Multi-Agent CI Pipeline I Built So I Could Ship Open Source Like a Team of One](https://joemattiello.dev/blog/multi-agent-ci-pipeline/) — Production multi-agent CI across the Provenance organization. Three agents (Claude, Cursor, Kimi), one dispatch vocabulary, reviewer-model auto-selection by static diff analysis, scheduled poller workaround for GitHub bot-actor gates.

## Press

- **April 2026 — [MobileGamer.biz](https://mobilegamer.biz/emulation-arrived-on-the-app-store-two-years-ago-60m-downloads-later-whats-changed/)** — Joseph is the featured developer interview in the 2-year App Store emulator retrospective. Quoted directly on App Store policy ("I've been pretty conservative about staying inside the lines from day one"), Provenance Plus IAP economics, and emulation rules.
- 9to5Mac, iMore (exclusive), Time Extension, Android Authority, AppleInsider, Pocket Gamer (dedicated hub), MacRumors emulator guide.
- YouTube coverage: TWiT (2.3M subs), ETA PRIME (2.2M), Spawn Wave, Retro Game Corps.
- Earlier (Sense Networks era, 2008-2010): Newsweek cover ("The Next Google"), Bloomberg Businessweek "25 Most Intriguing Startups", New York Times "Predicting Where You'll Go and What You'll Like", CNN Money.
